vulnerability_summary.title: Default JAAS denylist omits LdapLoginModule
vulnerability_summary.impact: An attacker who can control JAAS configuration can choose LdapLoginModule and bypass the intended default denylist, enabling outbound LDAP interaction.

code_facts.subject: DISALLOWED_LOGIN_MODULES_DEFAULT
code_facts.fact: The default denylist constant contains only JndiLoginModule in the vulnerable version.

code_facts.subject: throwIfLoginModuleIsNotAllowed
code_facts.fact: The denylist used by the check is read from System.getProperty(DISALLOWED_LOGIN_MODULES_CONFIG, DISALLOWED_LOGIN_MODULES_DEFAULT).

code_facts.subject: throwIfLoginModuleIsNotAllowed
code_facts.fact: The security check trims the login module name and rejects it only if disallowedLoginModuleList.contains(loginModuleName) is true.

patch_facts.subject: DISALLOWED_LOGIN_MODULES_DEFAULT
patch_facts.fact: The fix extends the default denylist constant by appending com.sun.security.auth.module.LdapLoginModule.

patch_facts.subject: JaasContextTest
patch_facts.fact: The fix adds regression tests asserting that LdapLoginModule is blocked by default and permitted only when the disallowed modules property is cleared or overridden.

environment_facts.fact: The exploit requires attacker control over a JAAS configuration source such as java.security.auth.login.config or sasl.jaas.config.
The vulnerable branch is the one where org.apache.kafka.disallowed.login.modules is not set to a safer override, so the default constant is used.

pattern_summary.summary: A security policy check exists, but the default denylist enforced by that check is incomplete because a required blocked member is missing.
pattern_summary.fix_strategy: Extend the default policy artifact to include the missing blocked member and add regression tests covering default and override branches.
